proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_078 (continuous, range 30-90) is positively associated with pfs_months in this cohort.
feature_057 (ordinal, levels 0-2) is negatively associated with pfs_months: higher levels predict shorter PFS.
feature_051 (binary) is associated with shorter pfs_months when present (=1) than when absent (=0).
feature_038 (binary) is associated with longer pfs_months when present (=1) than when absent (=0).

analyses.result_summary: Linear regression: slope=0.1765 months per unit feature_078, r=0.7661, p=0.000e+00. Strong positive association.
Linear regression on ordinal feature_057: slope=-1.1897 months/level (negative), r=-0.3516, p=0.000e+00. Means: lvl0=5.27, lvl1=4.05, lvl2=2.91.
T-test feature_051: mean(=1)=3.703 vs mean(=0)=5.055, diff=-1.352 months, p=0.000e+00.
T-test feature_038: mean(=1)=5.089 vs mean(=0)=4.117, diff=0.972 months, p=0.000e+00.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_099 (continuous, range 0-24.6) is negatively associated with pfs_months.
feature_092 (continuous, range 1.5-5.5) is positively associated with pfs_months.
feature_013 (binary) is associated with shorter pfs_months when =1 than when =0.
feature_043 (binary) is associated with shorter pfs_months when =1 than when =0.
feature_009 (continuous, range 0-777) is negatively associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Linear regression feature_099: slope=-0.07302 months/unit, r=-0.1229, p=2.207e-167.
Linear regression feature_092: slope=0.46536 months/unit, r=0.1018, p=3.249e-115.
Linear regression feature_009: slope=-0.00535 months/unit, r=-0.0377, p=3.131e-17.
T-test feature_013: mean(=1)=4.121 vs mean(=0)=4.448, diff=-0.327 months, p=8.528e-56.
T-test feature_043: mean(=1)=4.114 vs mean(=0)=4.419, diff=-0.305 months, p=5.226e-46.

proposed_hypotheses.text: pfs_months differs across feature_064 categories (white/hispanic/black/asian/other).
Among feature_064, black patients have shorter mean pfs_months than white patients (disparity).
pfs_months differs across feature_018 categories (medicare/private/medicaid/uninsured).
Within feature_018, uninsured patients have shorter mean pfs_months than privately insured patients.

analyses.result_summary: Black mean=4.273 vs white mean=4.316, diff=-0.042 months, p=1.872e-01.
Uninsured mean=4.249 vs private mean=4.330, diff=-0.082 months, p=1.267e-01.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable linear regression of pfs_months on feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, feature_038, feature_099, feature_092, feature_013, feature_043, feature_009, feature_109, feature_067, feature_064, and feature_018, feature_078 retains a significant positive coefficient.
In the same multivariable model, feature_051 retains a significant negative coefficient (i.e., adjusted effect is independent of other variables).
In the same multivariable model, feature_038 retains a significant positive coefficient (treatment-like effect).
In the same multivariable model, feature_057 retains a significant negative coefficient (per-level decrement in pfs_months).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_051 + feature_038 + feature_099 + feature_092 + feature_013 + feature_043 + feature_009 + feature_109 + feature_067 + C(feature_064) + C(feature_018)', data=df).fit().params['feature_078']
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient on feature_078 = 0.1758 (p=0.000e+00). Model R^2=0.8584.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_051 + feature_038 + feature_099 + feature_092 + feature_013 + feature_043 + feature_009 + feature_109 + feature_067 + C(feature_064) + C(feature_018)', data=df).fit().params['feature_051']
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient on feature_051 = -1.3614 (p=0.000e+00). Model R^2=0.8584.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_051 + feature_038 + feature_099 + feature_092 + feature_013 + feature_043 + feature_009 + feature_109 + feature_067 + C(feature_064) + C(feature_018)', data=df).fit().params['feature_038']
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient on feature_038 = 0.9428 (p=0.000e+00). Model R^2=0.8584.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_051 + feature_038 + feature_099 + feature_092 + feature_013 + feature_043 + feature_009 + feature_109 + feature_067 + C(feature_064) + C(feature_018)', data=df).fit().params['feature_057']
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient on feature_057 = -1.1715 (p=0.000e+00). Model R^2=0.8584.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The unadjusted gap in mean pfs_months between black and white patients (feature_064) is reduced toward zero after adjusting for clinical features (feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, feature_038, feature_099, feature_092).
The unadjusted gap in mean pfs_months between uninsured and privately insured patients (feature_018) is reduced toward zero after adjustment for the same clinical features.
After multivariable adjustment, the coefficient for black race (feature_064 = black) on pfs_months is not statistically significant.
After multivariable adjustment, the coefficient for uninsured (feature_018 = uninsured) on pfs_months is not statistically significant.

analyses.code: Compare unadjusted black-white diff to adjusted black-white diff in OLS
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted black-white diff = -0.042 months; adjusted (full model) black-white diff = 0.0090 months. Disparity attenuates to ~0 after adjustment.

analyses.code: Compare unadjusted uninsured-private diff to adjusted
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted uninsured-private diff = -0.082 months; adjusted = -0.0233 months.
Adjusted black coefficient (vs reference asian) = -0.0020, p=0.918. Not significant.
Adjusted uninsured coefficient (vs reference medicaid) = -0.0269, p=0.218.

feature_075 (ordinal 0-4) is negatively associated with pfs_months: each level decrements PFS.

feature_026 (ordinal 0-4) is negatively associated with pfs_months.
feature_096 (ordinal 0-4) is negatively associated with pfs_months.
feature_033 (ordinal 0-4) is negatively associated with pfs_months.

The benefit of feature_038 (=1 vs =0) on pfs_months is larger in patients with feature_057=0 than in patients with feature_057>=1 (effect modified by stage-like ordinal).
The effect of feature_038 on pfs_months differs by feature_092 level (interaction with continuous biomarker).
The effect of feature_038 on pfs_months differs by feature_051 status (binary biomarker interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_038 x feature_057: coef=-0.0568, p=1.158e-04. Negative = feature_038 benefit shrinks at higher feature_057.
Interaction feature_038 x feature_092: coef=-0.0855, p=1.914e-05.
Interaction feature_038 x feature_051: coef=0.0273, p=1.748e-01.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_051 (=1 vs =0) on pfs_months is larger in magnitude at higher feature_057 levels (interaction).
The negative effect of feature_051 on pfs_months differs across feature_064 race categories.
The effect of feature_051 on pfs_months differs by feature_078 level (interaction with continuous performance/age-like).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_051 x feature_057: coef=0.0467, p=1.840e-04.
Joint test of feature_051 x feature_064 interactions: stat=0.32238657509309165, p=0.8631450942817273.
Interaction feature_051 x feature_078: coef=-0.0071, p=1.131e-16.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_007 (binary) is associated with pfs_months: mean pfs_months differs between groups.
feature_067 (binary) is associated with longer pfs_months when =1 than when =0.
The effect of feature_038 (treatment-like) on pfs_months differs by feature_007 (subgroup heterogeneity).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_038 x feature_007: coef=0.0278, p=2.019e-01.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The relationship between feature_078 and pfs_months is nonlinear: a quadratic term significantly improves fit over a linear term.
Patients in the lowest tertile of feature_078 have shorter pfs_months than patients in the highest tertile.
The effect of feature_078 on pfs_months differs across feature_064 race categories (interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Quadratic feature_078^2 coefficient = 0.000654, p=2.551e-93.

analyses.code: ttest tertile low vs high feature_078
analyses.result_summary: Tertile low feature_078 mean=2.389, high tertile mean=6.266, diff=-3.877, p=0.000e+00.
Joint test of feature_078 x feature_064 interactions: stat=0.761, p=0.5506.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, feature_038, feature_092, feature_099, feature_009, and ordinal feature_025, feature_075, feature_071, feature_026, feature_096, feature_033, no significant disparity in pfs_months remains for feature_064 = black vs reference.
After the same adjustment, no significant disparity remains for feature_018 = uninsured vs reference.

analyses.code: OLS large model with race indicators
analyses.result_summary: Joint Wald p for feature_064 indicators in fully adjusted model: p=0.7461 (F=0.486); black coef=-0.0020, p=0.919. R^2=0.8584.

analyses.code: OLS large model with insurance indicators
analyses.result_summary: Joint Wald p for feature_018 indicators: p=0.6341 (F=0.571); uninsured coef=-0.0269, p=0.218.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean feature_078 differs across feature_064 race categories (race confounded with feature_078).
Mean feature_057 differs across feature_064 race categories.
Prevalence of feature_051 differs across feature_064 race categories.
Mean feature_092 differs across feature_064 race categories.
Among feature_064 race categories, the proportion receiving feature_038 (=1) differs (treatment access disparity).
Among feature_018 insurance categories, the proportion receiving feature_038 (=1) differs.
After adjusting for feature_078 and feature_057, the residual race effect on feature_038 receipt remains (logistic regression).

analyses.result_summary: feature_038 prop by race: {'asian': 0.211, 'black': 0.201, 'hispanic': 0.198, 'other': 0.192, 'white': 0.2}. chi2=3.05, p=5.502e-01.
feature_038 prop by insurance: {'medicaid': 0.198, 'medicare': 0.202, 'private': 0.201, 'uninsured': 0.19}. chi2=1.91, p=5.906e-01.
Joint Wald p for race indicators in feature_038 logit (after adjustment for feature_078, feature_057): p=0.5501.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A model including all top predictors plus ordinal/lab features explains substantially more variance in pfs_months (R^2 > 0.85) than the multivariable model from iteration 4.
Among the strong continuous predictors, feature_078 has the largest standardized (per-SD) effect on pfs_months.
Adding feature_038-by-feature_057 interaction to the multivariable model significantly improves fit (likelihood-ratio test).

analyses.code: compare R^2 of m4 (iter 4) vs m12 (iter 12)
analyses.result_summary: R^2 (iter 4 model) = 0.8584; R^2 (iter 12 expanded model) = 0.8584.

analyses.code: coefficients * SD for continuous predictors in m12
analyses.result_summary: Standardized (per-SD) effects on pfs_months: {'feature_078': np.float64(1.749938388345404), 'feature_092': np.float64(0.23531919775832127), 'feature_099': np.float64(-0.2935721472046545), 'feature_009': np.float64(-0.08095237584382212)}. feature_078 dominates with effect ~ 1.750 months per SD.
LRT statistic = 14.863, df=1, p=0.0001. Coef on feature_038:feature_057 = -0.0568.
